mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.91% as of 2022-11-15 indicates easing cycle initiation
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following tightening peak
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking markets reprice.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth rate decelerating below 0.5% month-over-month indicates supply-side weakness and margin compression across the economy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as profit expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or the relationship between INDPRO growth and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both declining simultaneously indicate broad-based demand destruction across residential and consumer sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The joint signal is stronger than either alone because it rules out sector-specific shocks.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,100K units AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,172K and RSAFS at 473,808M as of 2022-11-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 100K new jobs per month combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) signals labor market inflection point that increases recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146,135K and UNRATE at 4.3% as of 2022-11-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase in late-cycle downturn
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.10) when controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth rate above 8% combined with federal funds rate below 2% indicates real rates remain deeply negative, signaling continued monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in this specific regime.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 243.846 and FEDFUNDS at 0.91% as of 2022-11-15
regime_hint: high inflation with accommodative policy rates creating negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient reverses sign or becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 1% annualized rate signals late-cycle slowdown that compresses earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward guidance is reduced.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,861.6B as of 2022-07-15 (most recent quarterly data)
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
